mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases net present value of corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite unemployment spike, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and forward-looking consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both series to decline simultaneously to activate.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression of direction_20d on RSAFS and HOUST interaction, or joint decline events produce positive returns > 60% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 101.0 signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity gains that support corporate profit margins and earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns when INDPRO > 101.0 are not significantly higher than when INDPRO <= 101.0 (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint expansion in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and decline in unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals strong labor market momentum that increases consumer purchasing power and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both conditions to hold simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 100k month-over-month AND UNRATE falls > 0.1pp
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression, or joint labor strength events produce negative returns > 45% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) above 275.0 signals inflationary pressure that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain or raise the federal funds rate, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days in a monetary tightening regime.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns when both inflation and rates are elevated produce positive outcomes > 55% of the time, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in real GDP growth (GDPC1) below the prior quarter's level signals weakening economic momentum and forward earnings contraction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following quarter-over-quarter GDP declines are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in housing starts (HOUST) above 1700 thousand units signals strong residential investment demand and forward-looking economic confidence, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days in expansion regimes.
regime_hint: economic expansion with strong housing demand
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns when HOUST > 1700 are not significantly higher than when HOUST <= 1700 (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
